items.title: Child protection advocates slam decision to scrap SA social worker registration scheme

children.content: Child protection advocates say the South Australian government has let down the state's most vulnerable people by axing a mandatory registration scheme for social workers, but those in the DV sector say the move could help alleviate workforce shortages.

items.title: Gus Lamont's grandmother denies involvement in disappearance

children.content: One of the grandmothers of missing four-year-old Gus Lamont says police have a theory that she buried her grandson after an accident — an allegation she describes as "ludicrous".

items.title: Delays to alcohol delivery restrictions worry SA anti-violence advocates

children.content: Family violence prevention advocates say the SA government has yet to progress alcohol delivery legislation as recommended by last year's Royal Commission into Domestic, Family and Sexual Violence.

items.title: Tony Modra's family 'extremely grateful' for support after crash

children.content: The family of AFL icon Tony Modra has released a statement thanking the community for the "overwhelming show of love" they have received since he was seriously injured in a crash last week.

articles.title: 'Tony Modra is awake': Ricciuto describes optimism for star's recovery

children.content: Adelaide Crows fans wish the AFL great well in his recovery after surgery as former teammate Mark Ricciuto says he might not be as good-looking as he once was.

items.title: Thunderbirds first team through to Super Netball Grand Final

children.content: The Adelaide Thunderbirds are through to their third Super Netball grand final in four years. Now they wait to see if they will face the Melbourne Vixens or West Coast Fever for a shot at the title.

image.altText: Players hug and smile as they celebrate in a huddle

items.title: Russian timber getting into Australia by exploiting loopholes, industry says

children.content: Russian imports have faced tariffs since the start of the war in Ukraine, but timber is being rerouted through third countries and is entering Australia.

items.title: Pitched as an outback oasis, this remote town is 'actually reinventing itself'

items.title: Why these retirees won't give up the 3:30am starts and 14-hour shifts

items.title: Multiple SA voters who 'definitely voted' surprised by failure to vote notices

children.content: Multiple voters have received notices from the Electoral Commission of South Australia informing them they will be fined for not voting in the state election, despite having voted.

items.title: Adelaide casino operator fined $21m for 'completely unacceptable' failings

children.content: SkyCity Adelaide reaches a settlement agreement with South Australia's Liquor and Gambling Commissioner in what the premier says is possibly the biggest fine handed out in the state's history.

items.title: Man living with motor neurone disease keeps woodwork passion alive

articles.title: Messi's record-breaking World Cup goals make believers of us all

items.title: Security agencies issue blunt 'act swiftly' warning on AI

items.title: Iran to allow nuclear inspections, JD Vance says

items.title: Inspired by volcanoes, this new technology could affect billions of people

items.title: The Rosetta Stone was taken from Egypt. This artist took it back

children.content: The Rosetta Stone changed the way we understood Ancient Egypt. It sits in the British Museum but this artist wanted to return it home. To pull it off he needed some legal advice, a digital scanner and the courage to take on the way we look at history.

articles.title: What we know about how H5N1 bird flu got here and where it may appear next

items.title: Quick hits: Iran's incredible keeper and young Yamal starts in style

children.content: Young Lamine Yamal scored less than 10 minutes into his World Cup debut; Iran and Cabo Verde both held out for famous draws against highly-fancied opponents Belgium and Uruguay respectively; and Cabo Verde's most famous mum arrives to see her son. Here's the quick hits from the World Cup.

articles.title: Vegemite levels help Socceroos' chef find out if players like his food

children.content: Chef Vini Capovilla has learnt a thing or two catering for the Socceroos at four World Cups. And one is finding out if they like his food.

articles.title: The most culturally diverse World Cup ever meets Trump's America

articles.title: Egypt claims first ever World Cup win at New Zealand's expense
